tool.name: seqmatchall
tool.summary: report all-against-all maximal exact shared regions across a sequence set

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `seqmatchall` reports maximal exact ungapped shared regions for every record pair in one sequence set. EMBOSS-RS v1 compares each pair once in input order and emits every exact region meeting the minimum word size.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local sequence set plus an optional minimum word size. Molecule compatibility is enforced pairwise through the shared exact-word infrastructure.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The output is a deterministic table with left and right record identifiers, 1-based inclusive coordinates in both records, match length, and the exact shared region.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `emboss-rs seqmatchall`. Validation currently covers one committed three-record FASTA fixture whose three pairings each yield the same maximal 4-residue exact region.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release reports exact ungapped regions only. It does not perform heuristic seeding, gapped extension, or EMBOSS-era scoring beyond maximal exact region extraction.

artifacts.label: Seqmatchall multi-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed three-record FASTA fixture used for deterministic all-against-all exact-region reporting.

examples.title: Report all-against-all maximal exact regions
examples.description: Runs `seqmatchall` against a committed three-record FASTA fixture with a minimum word size of 4.

expected_outputs.label: All-against-all exact shared-region table
expected_outputs.description: Three pairwise rows are reported, each carrying the shared exact region `ACGT` with stable pair ordering.
